About:
Faz-Anim 2 is the successor to Faz-Anim 1, being a simulator for animatronic showtapes that allows users to program characters to any audio, video, or music.
	
	Unlike traditional 3D animation engines, the Give-LIFE engine inside Faz-Anim 2 mimics how traditional animatronics were programmed, having each pneumatically controlled movement on the character have a simple 'on' or 'off' state editable on a timeline. Unlike the prior RR-Engine, GL-Engine has built in support for custom states to allow changes to the audio/video tracks, colors, text, and 'float' values to be compatible with more types of classic animatronics that may be modded in.
	
	Each set of animatronics get their own collection of movements, lights, and special effects that can be animated, exported, and shared. Faz-Anim 2 also contains the characters from Faz-Anim 1, alongside the ability to convert FA1 showtape files into the new format for a seamless transition.
	
	The game also features robust and easy modding support, with it only taking a few minutes to drop in a character and have it become a working animatable bot.
Controls:
[font_size=26]In-Game[/font_size]
ESC = Pause
Tab = Toggle Editor
WASD = Move
Space = Jump
Q = Crouch
Shift = Sprint Speed
E = Toggle Flashlight
E (Hold) + Scroll = Change Flashlight Size

Ctrl (Hold) + Scroll = Change Camera Zoom
Alt (Hold) + Scroll = Change Camera Tilt

R = Smooth Out Movement Toggle
R (Hold) + Scroll = Change Smooth Amount

F = Smooth Out Camera Toggle
F (Hold) + Scroll = Change Smooth Amount

V = Fly
Flying + Q = Descend
Flying + Space = Ascend

H = Toggle Cosmetics (Temporary)

[font_size=26]In-Editor[/font_size]
P = Play/Pause Show (Works In-Game Too)
Shift + Scroll = Pan the Timeline
Ctrl + Scroll = Zoom the Timeline
Scroll = Scroll the Channel List

For each channel, the '[ ]' box on the left can be hovered and a number key (0-9) pressed to assign that key to the channel. Pressing BackSpace deletes that assignment.

When hovering a channel with an assigned key, holding that key and moving the mouse plots out a signal for that channel. You can also hit the play button and press those keys to have them plotted down along with the audio.

Right clicking any plotted signal deletes it. Left click dragging can move the signal. Left lcick dragging the edge of a signal can lengthen/shorten it.
Development Plans:
Faz-Anim 2 is currently in the early stages of development, and a lot is needed to be made before its ever ready to be completed- just as Faz-Anim 1 took years to finalize.
	
	The current set of plans are to have a fully fleshed out editor system that surpasses the RR-Engine. While this previously was in the form of a node system, the complexity confused users and this has been downscoped to a similar feeling editor to the RR-Engine. The new editor will eventually have full support for floats, colors, strings, videos, and audio on top of the current booleans. Each type will also have multiple ways your assigned bits can influence the track, such as modifying a color to be randomized as you hold down the button- or for floats to be transitioned to between points.
	
	FA2 also imports FA1 assets and plans on having the entirety of FA1 characters, props, and the map imported and touched up to be the definitive way of viewing FA1 tapes. Maybe the OG in-development FA1 map may be imported too.
	
	FA2's original assets plan on having two showrooms full of uniquely designed animatronics. FA2 will also have a game/story mode featuring night gameplay. Showtapes are planned to be playable inside night mode so players can have soundtracks to their playthroughs (or custom night attempts).
	
	The biggest hope and plan if everything can be executed well is to have proper 'accurate' maps/modes for all the major classic FNaF games and the movies. This was a want since Faz-Anim 1 but the scope was a bit crazy. Eventually I would hope (if this game got enough attention for people to care) to reach out to fangame devs to bring some of their characters in. Don't tell anybody but I'd kill for a Popgoes or Frickbears 3 map, FA1 got some amazing mods but I think the potential is endless.
Changes from Faz-Anim 1:
FA1 was an iteration of the RR-Engine made in Unity. FA2 is under the new Give-LIFE engine in Godot which supports more complex animatable showtapes, a more readable and compact showtape file format, and proper in-game mod support.

	FA1 had a focus on creating realistic but faithful iterations of the classic cast in an imagining of how they would look and perform in real life (before the FNaF movie had even come out). FA2 has a completely original set of designs and a focus on more complex programming for the characters. 

	FA1 had a focus on realism to the point of there being no ties to FNaF lore or haunted animatronics, taking its own spin with a player robot being used to program other robots. FA2 is completely separate from FA1 in lore and its plans do feature a closer FNaF style spooky story.

	FA1 had a ticket earning system where creating showtapes would allow prizes to be purchased. FA2 plans on this being done through completing each night/challenge.
